intro: Nehemiah (meaning consoled by the LORD), son of Hachaliah, was a Jewish cupbearer to the Persian king Artaxerxes I who received news of Jerusalem's broken walls and burned gates (Neh. 1:1–3). His prayer of confession and intercession (Neh. 1:5–11) was followed by his request to return and rebuild the city, which Artaxerxes granted in the 20th year of his reign (c. 445 B.C.). Against opposition from Sanballat and Tobiah and despite assassination plots, Nehemiah organized the Jews of Jerusalem and completed the rebuilding of the city walls in 52 days (Neh. 6:15). He served as governor of Judah for twelve years, during which he also worked with Ezra on covenant renewal and social reforms — enforcing Sabbath observance, dissolving mixed marriages, and ensuring the Levites' support (Neh. 13).
